question.text: CTT rate on commodity options is:

answer.text: Levied on the option premium — payable by the seller on exercise
Levied on the full contract value
The same rate as equity STT

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CTT on commodity options is levied on the option premium (not the full underlying value). It's payable by the seller when the option is exercised. **The Trap:** Not on full contract value. CTT does apply to options. Rates differ from STT. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers STT on equity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CTT on options is typically much lower than on futures (since it's on premium, not contract value) — this makes options relatively more cost-efficient for small positions.
